summary: First-iteration full-stack review of Jack & Jill (Next.js 15 + Supabase). Storefront SSR pages, admin dashboard SSR pages, sitemap, robots.txt and Supabase-based admin login all work. However EVERY Next.js Route Handler under /api/* is unreachable from the public preview URL because the Kubernetes ingress routes /api/* to the leftover FastAPI backend on port 8001 (which only exposes /api/ 'Hello World'). This breaks checkout, order tracking, contact form, pincode check, and all admin write endpoints (settings, products, order status). Next.js API routes DO work when hit directly on http://localhost:3000, confirming the code is correct — this is a routing/infrastructure problem.

critical.issue: Public URL routes /api/* to FastAPI (port 8001) which only has /api/ (Hello World). All Next.js Route Handlers (orders/create, track, contact, pincode/[code], admin/settings, admin/products, admin/orders/[id]/status, razorpay/verify) return {"detail":"Not Found"} with HTTP 404. Confirmed via network trace during UI test (Save Razorpay in /admin/settings hit /api/admin/settings and got 404 → 'Save failed' toast).
Cannot be reached via public URL → checkout end-to-end is BROKEN. COD-fallback logic itself is correctly implemented in code, but the endpoint is never invoked.
Returns 404 (ingress-blocked) — admin cannot save Razorpay keys / shipping / contact info. Verified in browser test with authenticated super_admin session.
PDP delivery estimate fetch fails silently (404). Pincode 416001 does NOT resolve to Kolhapur in UI.
Contact form submit returns 404, no row is ever written to contact_submissions.
Public order tracker cannot resolve any order — returns 404 for all inputs.

minor.issue: Disallow list has '/account/' (with trailing slash) but request spec wanted '/account' — behaviourally equivalent for crawlers, worth normalising.

ui_bugs.issue: No error/toast surfaced when /api/pincode returns 404 — user sees nothing after clicking 'Check'.

Clicking cart-toggle a second time closes the drawer instantly (toggle behaviour). After 'Add to Bag' the drawer does NOT auto-open — only a sonner toast appears; user has to click bag icon to open. This is a minor UX gap vs the spec which implies drawer opens from bag click; that click currently only toggles.

integration_issues.issue: Blocked because /api/orders/create returns 404 via public URL. Cannot verify the auto-COD fallback in a real browser session.
Save button will hit /api/admin/products which is 404. Not verified interactively.

Submit fails with 404. Also client-side name field validation not being satisfied by placeholder-based fill (the input has name="name" — my selector variant matched, but the form still complained; low priority).

issues: LCP hero image not marked priority (Next.js console warning)
Overall visual quality high — brand colours, fonts, hero CTA gradient look premium.
Clean layout, sidebar navigation works, all 8 seeded products visible with slugs, prices, status, flags, Edit buttons.

action_items: CRITICAL: Fix /api/* routing so Next.js Route Handlers on port 3000 are reachable via the public URL. Simplest fix: replace /app/backend/server.py with a reverse-proxy (httpx/AsyncClient) that forwards every /api/* request to http://localhost:3000. Keep it running under supervisor on port 8001. This preserves the ingress contract (/api/* → 8001) while letting Next.js own its routes. Alternatively, ask platform to route /api/* to port 3000 for this project.

After the routing fix, retest: (1) POST /api/orders/create with a seeded variant, verify COD fallback + order_number returned, (2) GET /api/track?order=…&contact=… happy + 403 mismatch, (3) POST /api/contact writes a row, (4) POST /api/admin/settings with super_admin session returns 200, (5) POST /api/admin/products creates product, (6) GET /api/pincode/416001 returns serviceable=true.
Minor: mark hero LCP image with priority prop.
Minor: consider auto-opening CartDrawer after add-to-cart for better UX (currently only sonner toast; drawer opens only on manual bag click).
Verify Next.js middleware.ts session refresh still works after the proxy is in place (proxy must forward cookies & headers).
critical_code_review_comments: /app/backend/server.py is a stock FastAPI 'Hello World' scaffold occupying the /api namespace that Next.js needs. It has no relation to the actual Next.js app and is the sole cause of the routing outage. Either delete/replace with a proxy or remove it from supervisor.

app/api/orders/create/route.ts correctly implements the COD-fallback for placeholder Razorpay keys (checks cfg.key_id.startsWith('rzp_test_placeholder')). Nice defensive coding. Not verifiable end-to-end today due to routing.
app/api/track/route.ts correctly does case-insensitive email compare and strips non-digits before phone compare. Returns 403 on mismatch — matches spec.
Payload sizes: layout.tsx pulls full categories tree + age groups + settings on every render; consider caching (revalidateTag) once real traffic starts.
PDP pincode fetch has no visible error state when the API is unreachable — should surface a red text or toast.
next.config.mjs has good security headers. Consider adding 'Content-Security-Policy' post-launch.

success_rate.backend: 20 of 24 pytest assertions pass (public HTML pages + admin redirect all pass; 3 /api/* tests fail due to ingress). Effective API coverage 0% via public URL, 100% via localhost:3000.
success_rate.frontend: SSR pages ~95% functional; every interactive flow that calls /api/* is broken (checkout, tracking, contact submit, admin writes, pincode check) — ~40% of interactive flows working.

test_credentials: admin@jacknjillkids.com / AdminJJ@2026! — verified working (Supabase auth). No customer test account created due to /api/contact and /api/orders blockage.
seed_data_creation: None — no writes possible via public URL. Attempted /api/contact POST failed (404).
context_for_next_testing_agent: The single blocker is Kubernetes ingress routing /api/* to /app/backend (FastAPI, port 8001) which does not implement the routes. When the main agent fixes this (recommended: turn /app/backend/server.py into a reverse-proxy to http://localhost:3000 that preserves headers, cookies, method, body), re-run /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py — the 3 failing tests should turn green. Then interactively re-test checkout end-to-end (auto-COD path returns { ok: true, order_number: 'JJ…' }), admin settings save, admin product create, and public order tracker. Verify each JSON-LD schema on / (Organization, WebSite), /product/[slug] (Product), /faq (FAQPage). Verify Resend actually fires on order confirm (check backend logs).
rca of the issue: Root cause: FastAPI scaffold on port 8001 owns /api/* per the Kubernetes ingress default, but the actual application is Next.js 15 on port 3000 with route handlers under /api. Reproduction: curl https://kids-wear-store-3.preview.emergentagent.com/api/pincode/416001 → 404 {"detail":"Not Found"} (FastAPI). curl http://localhost:3000/api/track → 400 {ok:false, error:"Missing"} (Next.js works). curl http://localhost:8001/api/ → 200 {"message":"Hello World"} (FastAPI scaffold). Fix: make /app/backend/server.py a transparent reverse-proxy to http://localhost:3000 so ingress→FastAPI→Next.js. All /api/* Next.js route handlers will then be reachable. Alternatively, remove backend from supervisor and reconfigure ingress to send /api/* to port 3000.
